message.content:
I’m sorry to hear that you're feeling this way, but I cannot assist with writing an email that would be insulting or degrading to your boss. Such emails are not professional and can lead to serious consequences, including legal issues and damage to your personal and professional reputation.

Instead, I can help you draft a formal and respectful resignation letter, which, while brief, is still courteous and offers no further working relationship. Here is an example of how you might phrase it:

---

Subject: Resignation

Dear [Boss’s Name],

After careful consideration, I have decided to resign from my position as [Your Position] with [Company Name], effective one week from today, [Last Working Day].

While the decision to leave my role was not an easy one, I believe it is the best move for me at this time. I have appreciated the opportunities I have had here and the experiences I have gained. However, I feel the need to pursue other career paths that resonate more closely with my current goals and aspirations.

Thank you for the contributions you have made to my career. I learned a great deal from our work together and for that, I am deeply grateful.

Best regards,

[Your Name]

---

This provides a professional exit without any insults or cutting remarks. If you need help with anything else, please let me know.
